title: Fraud — Federal Awards Disclosure
description: This policy governs how the school district detects, reports, and addresses fraud, with a specific focus on programs funded by federal money. It applies to district staff, administrators, and anyone involved in managing federal grant funds on behalf of the school. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it helps ensure that taxpayer and federal dollars intended for students are used honestly and appropriately, and that wrongdoing is properly disclosed and addressed.

timeline_local.summary: Revisions to Board Policy CDA (Local) were adopted unanimously at the November 18, 2025 Regular Board Meeting as part of a group adoption (Motion #7207). No specific language changes are described in the meeting minutes.
timeline_local.notes: Adopted as part of a group motion (#7207) along with BBE, CAA, CQB, EHB, EHBB, and GKB. Vote was 7-0. The specific redline changes are not described in these minutes pages.

timeline_local.notes: This is a table of contents / agenda slide (page 128) listing policies under 'Second Reading.' It does not contain redline text or specific policy language. Detailed changes would appear in subsequent pages of the packet for each individual policy.

timeline_local.summary: The Federal Awards Disclosure section is being updated to align with federal requirements. The changes clarify that the district, as an applicant, recipient, or subrecipient of a Federal award, must promptly disclose credible evidence of fraud, conflict of interest, bribery, or gratuity violations found in Title 18 of the U.S. Code or the civil False Claims Act. The revision removes the phrase 'in a timely manner' and replaces it with 'promptly,' expands the scope to include the agency's Office of Inspector General, and clarifies that disclosure applies to federal grant awards including activities or subawards thereunder.

The section affected is 'Federal Awards Disclosure.' The policy code is not explicitly stated on this page but context and triage suggest CDA. The changes appear to update federal award fraud disclosure language to match current federal regulatory requirements.

description_local: This policy governs how the school district detects, prevents, and reports fraud, with a specific focus on disclosing required information when the district applies for or receives federal grant funding. It applies to district administrators and staff who manage federal awards, but it also affects the broader community since federal funds support many school programs and services. Parents and community members should care about this policy because it helps ensure that taxpayer and federal dollars are used honestly and transparently, and that the district meets the legal requirements tied to receiving federal funding.
